channel.title: Rap Rock on Modest Walrus
channel.description: Recent content in Rap Rock on Modest Walrus

item.title: Reaction Numb/Encore by Linkin Park & JAY-Z
item.description:
Why I Reacted To This Song

Two acts that had no business sounding this good together somehow made it work, and the live performance takes it even further.

Original by Artist

About This Song

“Numb/Encore” arrived in 2004 as a genuinely unexpected pairing, a joint effort between JAY-Z and Linkin Park that blends JAY-Z’s sharp rap verses with Linkin Park’s atmospheric rock sound. The track came out of the Collision Course EP, a project built specifically to close the gap between two genres that rarely shared the same stage, mashing “Numb” and “Encore” into a single song rather than just trading verses over one beat.

item.title: Reaction Bulls On Parade by Rage Against The Machine
item.description:
Why I Reacted To This Song

There’s no truer words than what Zack de la Rocha lays down here. The anger is real and it’s pointed at something specific, not just noise for its own sake.

Original by Artist

About This Song

“Bulls on Parade” was released in 1996 on Rage Against the Machine’s second album, Evil Empire. Written by Zack de la Rocha and Tom Morello, the song takes aim at the military-industrial complex and the way arms manufacturing and war profiteering keep the machinery of conflict running. The guitar solo is one of the most recognizable in rock; Morello gets the vinyl-scratch sound by setting one pickup to full volume and killing the other, then rubbing his fretting hand across the strings over the dead pickup while flipping the selector switch like a killswitch. No turntable involved, just a guitar rewired to sound like one.
